solutions.solution: (d) According to the question. So, ⊗ is opposite to □, ⊡ is opposite to ⊞, and ⊞ is opposite to ⊞. Hence, only option (d) is the correct answer.
(d) According to the question. So, two blank faces of unfolded dice is opposite to another two blank faces. And ⊞ is opposite to ⊠. Clearly, option (a) is having the correct cubes.
(d) As per the question. So, ○ is opposite to △, ⊞ is opposite to ○, and ∇ is opposite to S. Now, it is clear that all cubes I, II, III and IV can be formed by folding this dice.
(d) According to the question. So, two blank faces are opposite to each other. Same as, ○ is opposite to ⊘, and ⊞ is opposite to □. Hence, only cube figure I i.e., option (a) is correct.
(d) According to the question. So, □ is opposite to ⊘. So, two blank faces are opposite to each other. And □ is opposite to ○. Hence, only figure II and IV are correct.
(d) According to the unfolded dice. So, ⊘ is opposite to □, and ⊘ is opposite to □. And two blank faces are opposite to each other. Now, it clear that figure I and IV can be formed.
(d) According to the unfolded dice. So, 1 is opposite to 3, 2 is opposite to 4, and 5 is opposite to 6. Clearly, Option (d) is the correct answer.
(d) When the question figure is folded into a box it will look like answer figure (d).
(b) When the question figure is folded into a box it will look like answer figure (b).
(c) Clearly, the similar figures will be on the opposite faces of one another.

(d) If we fold the option (a) the number 2 will lie opposite (5). If we fold the option (b) the number (1) will lie opposite (3). If we fold the option (c) the number (2) will lie opposite (5). Therefore, answer figure (d) is correct.
